source_jobs.title: BCBA Practice Owner - Launch and Grow your practice
source_jobs.description:
Launch and Grow Your Own ABA Practice — with Zero Upfront Costs

Join 3Y Health and earn $100,000 - $320,000/year while making your own schedule.

Tired of rigid clinical environments and professional burnout? Looking to build a practice and don’t know where to start? At 3Y Health, we believe BCBAs deserve more — more freedom, more support, and more impact. That’s why we’ve created a platform where you can launch and grow your own ABA practice with no upfront costs and full operational support.

We handle the logistics. You focus on care.

For more information, please visit: https://3yhealth.com/specialties/autism-therapy-aba

What Makes 3Y Health Different?

 - $0 upfront to join — No fees, no hidden costs.

 - Earn up to $320,000/year based on your client caseload and schedule.

 - Concierge assistance with LLC creation, business banking, and liability insurance.

 - Total flexibility — You choose your hours, clients, and care model.

 - World-class support — We take care of operations so you can focus on clinical outcomes.

 - Custom referral support to help you build your preferred caseload.

What We Provide:

 - All-in-one software suite – Includes Practice Management, Scheduling, EHR, Data Collection, Billing, Onboarding, and mobile apps for you, your RBTs, and your clients.

 - Insurance partnerships – We’re in-network with 100+ payers at high market rates, giving you broad access to patients.

 - Practice launch support – We’ll help you start your own ABA company if you don’t already have one.

 - Fast payments – Get paid upfront per session. No more chasing down insurance.

 - Payroll made easy – We’ll run payroll for you and your RBTs if you’d like.

Your Role:

 - Own and operate your practice – Set your hours, select clients, and define how you deliver care.

 - Hire and lead your RBTs – Build a team that aligns with your vision.

 - Deliver exceptional ABA therapy – You’re the expert. We give you the platform to thrive.

Why BCBAs Love 3Y Health:

 - 100% autonomy with zero admin headaches

 - Real earning potential with no financial risk

 - Flexible work-from-anywhere model

 - Make a bigger impact without burning out

source_jobs.title: Speech Language Pathology Consultant  (Florida)
source_jobs.description:
What We're Looking For:

We’re expanding our Speech Therapy services and need Speech-Language Pathologists (SLPs) to serve as part‑time consultants. You will help us shape local care models, expand insurance contracts, and if interested, create a launch‑pad to build or grow your own independent practice—without the usual operational headache.

Key Responsibilities:

 - We are looking for a variable commitment, up to 1 hr commitment per week

 - Advise on state regulations, cultural nuances, and care‑model innovation.

What We Provide:

 - Fully integrated practice‑management suite, payer enrollment team, and rapid‑pay billing.

 - Access to a nationwide peer community

 - Priority eligibility for a full practice‑owner track with 3Y Health whenever you’re ready.

Ideal Candidate:

 - Active SLP license in good standing in Florida

 - Familiarity with payer credentialing or utilization‑management processes is a plus.

Compensation will be $100-150 per hour dependent on experience and geographic location.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Frontend
source_jobs.description:
About Us

At 3Y Health, we are building AI-driven software to empower healthcare providers and solve the overwhelming administrative complexity that consumes 40% of the industry’s revenue. Our end-to-end platform unlocks opportunities for clinician entrepreneurs, enabling medical professionals to launch, run, and grow private practices. By supporting these independent practices with the latest AI and automation, we’re helping providers reclaim their time, build thriving businesses, and deliver better outcomes for their communities. 3Y Health is backed by over $200M from top-tier investors including Founders Fund, General Catalyst, Softbank, and 8VC.

About the Role

We are seeking a Frontend Engineer to help us craft intuitive, high-performance user interfaces that our providers love. The ideal candidate is a product-minded engineer with deep expertise in modern frontend development (React, TypeScript) and a strong sense of design and user interaction quality. You care about clean, modular code, and can collaborate closely with design and product to bring elegant experiences to life. Bonus points if you have light full-stack capabilities and can own backend tweaks to unblock your team. Ideal candidates may have experience as a founding engineer or founder and bring a bias toward ownership, speed, and continuous iteration.

This role requires a dynamic individual who can drive frontend architecture decisions and work cross-functionally across engineering, design, and product teams. As a Frontend Engineer, you will be responsible for delivering scalable, modular UI components, collaborating on product development, and shaping the user experience of our AI-first platform.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and maintain reusable, performant, and accessible frontend components using React and TypeScript

 - Work closely with designers to bring beautiful, user-centered experiences to life

 - Own UX polish and interaction quality; continuously seek ways to improve usability

 - Collaborate with backend engineers to define API interfaces and occasionally implement backend logic as needed

 - Influence frontend architectural decisions and promote best practices in code modularity and maintainability

 - Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and team rituals

 - Bring a founder’s mindset—proactive, product-focused, and eager to take ownership

Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree required

 - 3+ years of experience in frontend engineering roles

 - Deep expertise in React, TypeScript, modern JavaScript, and frontend architecture

 - Track record of delivering high-quality UI/UX in close collaboration with design/product

 - Ability to write clean, modular, scalable code with an eye for reusability

 - Bonus: experience with backend technologies (Node.js, Express, GraphQL, or similar)

 - Bonus: past experience as a founder, co-founder, or founding engineer

 - Ability to work 5 days a week in our San Francisco with a fully in-person team

Compensation 

The estimated salary range for this role is $175,000 - $205,000. Total compensation for this position may also include stock options. Note that total compensation for this position will be determined by each individual’s relevant qualifications, work experience, skills, and other factors.

source_jobs.title: Prior Authorization Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Us

At 3Y Health, we are building AI-driven software to empower healthcare providers and solve the overwhelming administrative complexity that consumes 40% of the industry’s revenue. Our end-to-end platform unlocks opportunities for clinician entrepreneurs, enabling medical professionals to launch, run, and grow private practices. By supporting these independent practices with the latest AI and automation, we’re helping providers reclaim their time, build thriving businesses, and deliver better outcomes for their communities. 3Y Health is backed by over $200M from top-tier investors including Founders Fund, General Catalyst, Softbank, and 8VC.

About the Role

We are seeking a Prior Authorizations Lead to design, manage, and scale 3Y Health’s prior authorization operations. The ideal candidate will be an operational problem-solver who thrives on efficiency and loves turning complexity into clarity. This role requires a strategic yet hands-on operator who can build scalable systems, streamline workflows, and ensure timely, accurate approvals for our clinician partners across multiple specialties.

As the Prior Authorizations Lead, you will own the end-to-end authorization process — from intake and submission to payer follow-up and resolution — ensuring fast turnaround times and exceptional partner experience. You’ll also partner closely with Product, RCM, and Operations teams to leverage automation and process design that reduce administrative burden for providers.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the end-to-end prior authorization process, including verification, documentation, submission, and follow-up with payers.

 - Build and optimize workflows that minimize turnaround times and maximize approval rates across multiple specialties.

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to identify automation opportunities and develop tools that reduce manual work.

 - Collaborate with RCM and Operations teams to ensure clean handoffs between authorizations, billing, and patient care coordination.

 - Develop and track KPIs to monitor authorization performance, identify bottlenecks, and continuously improve process efficiency.

 - Train and manage a growing team or vendor partners to ensure consistent execution and adherence to payer guidelines.

 - Maintain up-to-date knowledge of payer requirements, clinical criteria, and regulatory changes that impact authorization processes.

 - Build documentation, playbooks, and SOPs to support scaling into new states, payers, and clinical verticals.

Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree required

 - 5–8 years of experience in healthcare operations, prior authorization management, or related RCM functions

 - Strong understanding of payer requirements, medical necessity documentation, and authorization workflows

 - Proven ability to lead cross-functional initiatives and manage complex, high-volume processes

 - Analytical and systems-oriented thinker with a track record of driving measurable improvements in turnaround times and accuracy

 - Experience with automation tools or EMR/EHR integrations a plus

 - Ability to work 5 days a week in our San Francisco or New York office with a fully in-person team

Compensation

The estimated salary range for this role is $100,000–$140,000. Total compensation for this position may also include stock options. Note that total compensation for this position will be determined by each individual’s relevant qualifications, work experience, skills, and other factors.

source_jobs.title: AI Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Us

At 3Y Health, we are building AI-driven software to empower healthcare providers and solve the overwhelming administrative complexity that consumes 40% of the industry’s revenue. Our end-to-end platform unlocks opportunities for clinician entrepreneurs, enabling medical professionals to launch, run, and grow private practices. By supporting these independent practices with the latest AI and automation, we’re helping providers reclaim their time, build thriving businesses, and deliver better outcomes for their communities. 3Y Health is backed by over $200M from top-tier investors including Founders Fund, General Catalyst, Softbank, and 8VC.

About the Role

We’re seeking an AI Product Designer to design an Agentic system and AI agents for healthcare. The ideal candidate is a product-minded, hands-on designer with deep experience designing LLM/agent interactions (tool use, function calling, RAG, evals) and strong systems-thinking/design-ops chops (ideally with founding-designer experience at a YC/venture-backed startup). This role calls for a dynamic product design leader who can drive end-to-end delivery of agentic workflows and align cross-functional partners across sales, operations, marketing, recruiting, and product. As an AI Product Designer, you will own design strategy, information architecture, and execution of the agent platform and design system.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design AI interfaces and agent workflows (chat, tools, actions) end-to-end—from problem framing and user flows to high-fidelity Figma and production QA.

 - Collaborate with AI engineers to design interfaces that handle real-time data and dynamic outputs.

 - Prototype rapidly using Figma (advanced prototyping), React, Replit, and Cursor; when helpful, use lightweight code in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript or Framer; run usability tests, instrument analytics, and iterate based on controlled experiments and session reviews.

 - Simplify complex lab tasks into intuitive layouts and user flows.

 - Drive innovation by exploring new AI design opportunities and creating 0→1 designs that push the limits of creative AI applications.

 - Contribute to the long-term vision of AI at 3Y, shaping the roadmap and scaling AI capabilities across the company.

Qualifications

 - 7+ years in product design (5+ in complex B2B/enterprise); track record shipping AI-powered features or intelligent interfaces at scale.

 - Founding designer (YC or venture-backed) experience strongly preferred; portfolio showing systems thinking, data-dense UI, conversational patterns, and a cohesive design system with measurable impact.

 - Expert Figma (design tokens, components, advanced prototyping); comfortable with rapid interactive prototyping (HTML/CSS/JS or equivalent).

 - Fluency in LLM/agent concepts (prompt patterns, tool use/function calling, RAG trade-offs, evals) and how they inform UX and product design.

 - Skilled in product analytics, experimentation, and AI-augmented design workflows (ChatGPT/Claude, Midjourney/DALL·E, Copilot).

 - Experience working with cross-functional teams.

 - Ability to work 5 days a week in our New York office with a fully in-person team.

 

Compensation 

The estimated salary range for this role is $135,000 - $175,000. Total compensation for this position may also include stock options. Note that total compensation for this position will be determined by each individual’s relevant qualifications, work experience, skills, and other factors.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Backend
source_jobs.description:
About Us

At 3Y Health, we are developing an AI business manager to streamline healthcare administration and address the complexity that consumes nearly 40 percent of the industry’s revenue. Our comprehensive platform empowers clinician entrepreneurs to launch, manage, and grow their own private practices. By equipping independent providers with advanced AI and automation tools, we help them save time, build successful businesses, and deliver better care to their communities. 3Y Health is supported by over $200 million in funding from leading investors including Founders Fund, General Catalyst, SoftBank, and 8VC.

About the Role

We are seeking a Backend Engineer to design and scale high-performance backend systems that power our AI-first platform. The ideal candidate has experience building distributed services in languages such as Go, Java, Python, or TypeScript. You should be deeply familiar with modern API design and communication protocols (REST, gRPC, GraphQL), and have a passion for delivering reliable, secure, and scalable infrastructure.

Ideally, you’ve worked on services that support AI functionality—whether through integrating external AI APIs, managing ML inference pipelines, or supporting data infrastructure for model training. You're not just a code contributor—you care about how your systems are deployed, monitored, and managed in production. Bonus points if you’ve worked with regulated data environments like healthcare or fintech.

This role requires a hands-on engineer who thrives in cross-functional environments, collaborates effectively with product and frontend teams, and proactively builds for scale, compliance, and maintainability.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and implement scalable backend services and APIs (REST, gRPC, GraphQL) that power our AI-driven healthcare platform

 - Collaborate with frontend, product, and design teams to translate product requirements into robust technical solutions

 - Integrate AI capabilities into services—building pipelines, managing inference, or orchestrating external AI APIs

 - Ensure service reliability by implementing best practices in deployment, monitoring, observability, logging, and continuous integration

 - Operate production systems end-to-end, including deployment, incident response, performance tuning, and cost optimization

 - Handle sensitive and regulated data (healthcare, payments) with strong attention to security, privacy, and compliance

 - Document technical process and create standard operating procedure within the engineering team

 - Drive backend architecture decisions, influencing system design, data models, and service interfaces

 - Mentor and elevate team capability, sharing best practices and helping to scale backend engineering excellence

Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree required

 - 5+ years of experience in backend engineering roles

 - Proficiency in one or more backend languages: Go, Java, Python, TypeScript

 - Deep knowledge of modern API protocols: REST, gRPC, GraphQL

 - Experience supporting production systems end-to-end: development, deployment, monitoring

 - Bonus: exposure to AI/ML services, whether through API integration or internal tooling

 - Bonus: experience with regulated data domains like healthcare or financial services

 - Ability to work 5 days a week in our San Francisco office with a fully in-person team

Compensation 

The estimated salary range for this role is $205,000-$250,000. Total compensation for this position may also include stock options. Note that total compensation for this position will be determined by each individual’s relevant qualifications, work experience, skills, and other factors.

Why This Role Exists

We’re expanding our Occupational Therapy services and need Occupational Therapists (OTs) to serve as part‑time consultants. You will help us shape local care models, expand insurance contracts, and if interested, create a launch‑pad to build or grow your own independent practice—without the usual operational headache.

Key Responsibilities

 - We are looking for a variable commitment, up to 1 hr commitment per week

 - Advise on state regulations, cultural nuances, and care‑model innovation.

 - Advise on policies and outcome metrics to ensure evidence‑based, family‑centered care.

 - Act as the primary credentialed provider for the group when we are applying to commercial and Medicaid plans in your state.

 - Use and critique our all‑in‑one tech stack (scheduling, data, EHR, billing) to guide product improvements.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Us

At 3Y Health, we are building AI-driven software to empower healthcare providers and solve the overwhelming administrative complexity that consumes 40% of the industry’s revenue. Our end-to-end platform unlocks opportunities for clinician entrepreneurs, enabling medical professionals to launch, run, and grow private practices. By supporting these independent practices with the latest AI and automation, we’re helping providers reclaim their time, build thriving businesses, and deliver better outcomes for their communities. 3Y Health is backed by over $200M from top-tier investors including Founders Fund, General Catalyst, Softbank, and 8VC.

About the Role

We’re looking for a Forward Deployed Engineer to help build the infrastructure powering intelligent, agentic systems for healthcare. In this role, you'll focus on architecting reliable, scalable services and data pipelines that support AI agents, real-time workflows, and complex operational logic. You won’t need to build models yourself, but you’ll work closely with AI engineers and product designers to integrate LLM capabilities into production systems. This is a hands-on Individual Contributor (IC) role for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, enjoys building clean APIs, and wants to shape the foundational backend systems of a high-impact, mission-driven product.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design AI interfaces and agent workflows (chat, tools, actions) end-to-end—from problem framing and user flows to high-fidelity Figma and production QA.

 - Collaborate with AI engineers to design interfaces that handle real-time data and dynamic outputs.

 - Prototype rapidly using Figma (advanced prototyping), React, Replit, and Cursor; when helpful, use lightweight code in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript or Framer; run usability tests, instrument analytics, and iterate based on controlled experiments and session reviews.

 - Simplify complex lab tasks into intuitive layouts and user flows.

 - Drive innovation by exploring new AI design opportunities and creating 0→1 designs that push the limits of creative AI applications.

 - Contribute to the long-term vision of AI at 3Y, shaping the roadmap and scaling AI capabilities across the company.

 

Qualifications

 - 5+ years of backend engineering experience, ideally building production systems for complex or data-heavy applications. 

 - Strong experience with modern backend frameworks and languages (Java, Python, TypeScript/Node.js). 

 - Experience designing scalable APIs and distributed systems architecture. 

 - Solid understanding of database design (PostgreSQL, Redis, etc.), data modeling, and performance optimization.

 - Comfortable working in a startup environment with evolving priorities and high ownership expectations. 

 - (Preferred) Exposure to LLM workflows, agentic design patterns, or function calling APIs — or an interest in learning. 

 - (Bonus) Experience with healthcare data, compliance concerns (e.g., HIPAA), or operational tooling is a plus. Based in NYC or willing to work full-time in our NYC office with a fully in-person team.

 - Experience working with cross-functional teams.

 - Ability to work 5 days a week in our San Francisco and New York office with a fully in-person team.

 

Compensation 


The estimated salary range for this role is $250,000 - $300,000. Total compensation for this position may also include stock options. Note that total compensation for this position will be determined by each individual’s relevant qualifications, work experience, skills, and other factors.
